name: High-Protein Tiramisu
description: A no-bake high-protein tiramisu: a mascarpone-style cream of BLENDED 2% cottage cheese + 0% Greek yogurt, thickened with vanilla SF instant pudding, sweetened with granulated Splenda, layered with coffee-dipped ladyfingers and set overnight. Now the only tiramisu — the rice-cake version was retired (worse texture; rice cakes not on hand) after folding its one good idea, a Splenda-sweetened pudding cream, into this one. House calibration: sweetener is granulated Splenda, sweetness-matched (small maltodextrin calories counted); eggs medium. Tip: for a caramel-coffee twist, swap the vanilla pudding for butterscotch.

steps.instructionText: Blend the protein, cottage cheese, Greek yogurt, vanilla pudding mix, Splenda, salt, and cinnamon until completely silky — the cottage cheese MUST blend out with no grain so it reads as mascarpone. Taste and adjust the Splenda (the cottage cheese and 0% yogurt are tangy, so the cream needs a real hit of sweetness), then let it rest about 10 minutes so the instant-pudding starch firms it to a thick, spoonable mascarpone-style cream.

Pour strong coffee into a shallow bowl. Dip each ladyfinger just about 1 second per side and roll it out — do NOT soak (oversoaked ladyfingers turn to mush, and they keep hydrating overnight). Line a dish, spread half the cream, and dust with cinnamon. Repeat with the remaining dipped ladyfingers and cream.

Dust the top with cocoa and refrigerate OVERNIGHT — at least 6 hours, ideally 24 — so the cream sets firm and the ladyfingers hydrate into cake. This set is what makes it tiramisu instead of pudding. Slice into 9 and serve cold.

notes.text: One second per side is the maximum dip — ladyfingers keep absorbing moisture overnight. Dip too long and by morning they're waterlogged mush, not soft cake. Use a timer if in doubt.

Blend on high for at least 60 seconds and scrape the sides once — the 226 g (8 oz) cottage cheese needs full processing to lose all grain. Taste the cream; if you still feel texture, blend another 30 seconds.
Overnight is the minimum (6 hours absolute floor) — the instant pudding starch needs time to firm the cream from spoonable to sliceable. Cutting at 3–4 hours gives a collapsing, pudding-soft result.

The cream is tangy from cottage cheese and 0% yogurt; taste after blending and adjust Splenda before assembling — it should taste distinctly sweet since the ladyfingers and coffee add bitterness that mutes sweetness overnight.

The 40 g SF vanilla pudding mix does double duty: it sweetens and thickens. Let the blended cream rest 10 min before spreading — the starch activates and it firms to a thick, spreadable mascarpone consistency.
